name: Toyota Financial Services
description: As a React Native Developer supporting Toyota Financial Services, I contribute to a shared mobile platform powering multiple financial-services brands. My work includes developing customer-facing features, improving application performance, integrating analytics, and collaborating with product, UX, QA, and backend teams to deliver reliable mobile experiences. I also developed internal automation tooling that streamlined build and deployment workflows, reducing manual effort and improving release consistency across mobile applications.

projects.title: Collections & Loss Mitigation Features
projects.description: Implemented production features supporting collections and loss-mitigation workflows within financial-services applications. Collaborated across cross-functional teams to translate business requirements into scalable mobile solutions while maintaining code quality and platform consistency.

projects.title: Responsive Mobile UI Improvements
projects.description: Improved application responsiveness and consistency across different device sizes and operating systems. Addressed UI edge cases, layout inconsistencies, and platform-specific issues to deliver a more polished and reliable user experience.

projects.title: Payments Experience Enhancements
projects.description: Contributed to customer-facing payment workflows by implementing new functionality, resolving production issues, and improving overall user experience. Worked closely with product owners, UX designers, QA engineers, and backend teams to ensure seamless feature delivery across multiple mobile applications.

projects.title: Build & Deployment Automation
projects.description: Designed and implemented a Bash-based automation framework to streamline mobile release workflows. The solution automated version management, application packaging, artifact naming, Sauce Labs uploads, source-control updates, and release tracking. What previously required multiple manual steps could now be executed through a single command, improving deployment consistency and reducing release preparation effort.

name: Frontier Communications
description: As a React Native Team Lead at Frontier Communications, I led a team of developers delivering mobile features and enhancements for customer-facing applications. My responsibilities included technical guidance, performance optimization, production support, stakeholder collaboration, and ensuring successful feature delivery across the software development lifecycle.

projects.title: Release Management & Quality Initiatives
projects.description: Supported release planning, deployment activities, and quality initiatives for mobile applications. Worked with stakeholders to prioritize deliverables, coordinate releases, and improve overall application stability.

projects.title: Production Monitoring & Issue Resolution
projects.description: Utilized Firebase, Logstash, and Quantum Metric to investigate production issues, analyze user behavior, monitor application health, and support troubleshooting efforts. Collaborated with QA and engineering teams to identify root causes and deliver fixes efficiently.

projects.title: CMS Migration Support
projects.description: Contributed to content-management related enhancements and platform modernization efforts. Worked closely with cross-functional teams to ensure smooth integration, testing, and delivery of updated functionality while minimizing impact on existing user workflows.

projects.title: Application Performance Optimization
projects.description: Improved application performance through lazy loading, optimized rendering strategies, and enhanced fallback handling. These improvements reduced unnecessary re-renders, improved screen responsiveness, and contributed to a smoother user experience.

name: Insignia Consultancy Solutions
description: Insignia specializes in technology solutions for digital transformation. As a software development intern on the data science team, I work closely with large language models and Python programming. My role involves creating integrated solutions using tools like Hugging Face, OpenAI, Groq API, and Assembly AI. Despite having no prior AI background, I've successfully contributed to three major projects in just two months, including a Sentence Analyzer and real-time speech-to-text pipeline.

projects.title: Realtime Speech to Text Pipeline
projects.description: After delivering the previous project of STT pipeline, I worked on a realtime audio transcription system using Flask and various large language models (LLMs). The transcription process begins by capturing audio input, which is then processed to detect silence and manage recording efficiently. The system integrates multiple transcription services, applying robust error handling and retry mechanisms for API calls to ensure reliability. Additionally, it supports offline transcription using the Vosk framework. This project showcases my ability to integrate diverse technologies, implement complex workflows, and maintain a clean architecture while ensuring secure operations through logging and environment management.

projects.title: Speech to Text Pipeline
projects.description: Here, I developed a speech-to-text pipeline using Flask that enables users to record their voice and convert it to text with various large language models (LLMs). The project includes over nine core files, with pipeline.py as the main entry point. It features main.py for routing, start.py and stop.py for recording control, and config.py for managing model configurations and API keys. The transcription is handled in process.py, while a .env file stores critical parameters securely. I implemented centralized logging in logger.py to ensure comprehensive session tracking. This project showcases my expertise in building complex systems from scratch, utilizing technologies like Flask and external libraries for audio processing, along with a clear architectural design illustrated in a high-level flowchart.

projects.title: Sentence Analyzer
projects.description: In this project, I utilized Python to extract key travel-related information from sentences, leveraging powerful libraries like SpaCy for natural language processing and FuzzyWuzzy for string matching. The code was designed to identify source and destination locations, dates, and times from diverse texts, using regex patterns to capture various date and time formats, including relative references like 'tomorrow.' Additionally, I expanded the sentence analyzer to extract event names, guest counts, durations, and more, making the tool robust and versatile for applications in travel, event planning, and beyond. With over 1,500 lines of code, I'm proud of how this tool streamlines the process of gathering and organizing critical information, enhancing efficiency for users in both domains.

name: New Jersey Institute of Technology
description: At New Jersey Institute of Technology (NJIT), a top research university in Newark, I worked part-time as a Teaching Assistant while pursuing my MS in Computer Science. I first assisted with CS 656: Internet and Higher Layer Protocols, then returned the following semester for CS 630: Operating Systems Design. In both roles, I supported students with coursework, labs, and projects, deepening my technical knowledge while contributing to NJIT’s research-driven academic environment.

projects.title: TA for CS630: Operating Systems Design
projects.description: For CS 630 I had a class of more than 100 to handle with help of one more TA. Here I assisted the professor in assessing class participation, which was something newly introduced by me and my partner TA. In the assessing process we would keep track of each student who is answering professor's question, or actively asking doubts and being proactive in interacting with on going topics of studies. I also assisted in assessing midterm and final exams while also supervising them to ensure there was no misconduct or cheating taking place.

projects.title: TA for CS656: Internet and Higher Layer Protocols
projects.description: As a Teaching Assistant for CS 656: Internet and Higher Layer Protocols at NJIT, I played a key role in both designing and delivering course materials. Before being officially hired, I worked with the professor to develop a set of evaluation methods, including three mini-projects and one major project. These projects were designed to help students gain practical experience with various layers of network protocols, utilizing tools such as NS2, Cisco Packet Tracer, and client-server programming in Python. For the final major project, students were tasked with transforming their third mini-project into a globally hosted chat server using ngrok or other third-party platforms. In addition to designing projects, my responsibilities included explaining project concepts, assisting students with lab work, and providing guidance on course material. I also supported grading for over 40 students, evaluating lab assignments, projects, and exams, ensuring timely and constructive feedback to help students improve their understanding of networking concepts. This experience allowed me to strengthen my communication and technical skills while contributing to a collaborative learning environment.

name: Mentease
description: Mentease is a digital media and content creation company focused on producing engaging content for multiple clients. As a Video Content Specialist, I created motion graphics, video edits, promotional content, and social media assets, collaborating with stakeholders to transform ideas into visually compelling stories for digital platforms.

name: Webtraffic Agency
description: Webtraffic Agency is a digital marketing and branding agency serving clients across multiple industries. As a Graphic Designer, I created social media creatives, motion graphics, marketing campaigns, brand assets, and promotional videos. I collaborated with content creators, marketers, and clients to deliver visually engaging designs that strengthened brand identity and improved audience engagement.

name: Giro Fintech - UAE
description: Giro Fintech is a technology company focused on digital products and software solutions. As a Game Development Trainee, I worked on interactive web-based and mobile gaming projects, gaining hands-on experience in game mechanics, UI design, 3D assets, animation workflows, and gameplay implementation. This role helped strengthen my problem-solving skills and provided an early foundation in software development and user experience design.

name: TakenMind
description: TakenMind is an analytics and technology-focused organization that provides opportunities for students and early-career professionals to gain practical experience in data-driven decision making. As a Data Analytics & Visualization Intern, I worked with datasets to identify trends, create visualizations, and communicate insights through reports and dashboards. This experience strengthened my analytical thinking, data interpretation, and presentation skills.
